name: IBM i MCP Server
what_it_does: Official IBM MCP server for IBM i (formerly AS/400 and iSeries) — IBM's integrated operating system combining DB2 database, RPG and CL programming environments, job queuing, and system management. Enables AI agents to query DB2 for i databases, manage IBM i jobs, access system objects, run CL commands, and interact with IBM i application layers through MCP tool calls.
use_cases: Querying IBM i DB2 databases from data analysis agents
Managing IBM i jobs and job queues from operations agents
Running CL (Control Language) commands from system administration agents
Accessing IBM i file system and program objects from integration agents
Automating IBM i operations and monitoring from DevOps agents
not_for: Teams without IBM i infrastructure (highly specialized enterprise platform)
General database access outside the IBM i ecosystem
Organizations that haven't already invested in IBM i/AS400 infrastructure
best_when: An enterprise with existing IBM i/AS400 infrastructure wants AI agents to automate operations, query DB2 data, and manage IBM i system resources — official IBM MCP provides authoritative coverage of the platform.
avoid_when: You don't have IBM i infrastructure — this is specialized for organizations maintaining AS/400/iSeries systems, primarily in manufacturing, finance, and government sectors.

auth.notes: IBM i credentials required (user profile and password). Connects via IBM i Access Client Solutions or direct ODBC/JDBC. IBM i user profile permissions control what operations are accessible.

pricing.paid_tiers: IBM i software licensing; server hardware costs significant
pricing.notes: IBM i platform licensing is expensive (IBM proprietary). MCP server itself is free open source from IBM. Only relevant for organizations already running IBM i infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: IBM i system resources and connection pool limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: IBM i concepts (jobs, job queues, CL commands) require IBM i expertise to use effectively
DB2 for i has SQL extensions not found in standard DB2 or other databases
CL command execution is powerful and can affect production systems — gate carefully
IBM i security model (user profiles, object authority) must be understood before deployment
Official IBM MCP — high quality and actively maintained by IBM engineers
agent_readiness.security_notes: IBM i user profile and password required. IBM i's object-level security system controls all access. CL commands can modify system state — restrict to read-only operations for autonomous agents. Enterprise-grade security model with comprehensive audit logging.

security_score_components.security_notes: IBM i enterprise security model. User profile/password auth with object-level authorization. Official IBM MCP. CL commands can affect production — use read-only profiles for autonomous agents.
